title: Authorised Access Control
description: Limit information system access to authorised users, processes acting on behalf of authorised users, and devices (including other information systems).
purpose: Ensures only authorised individuals and processes can access federal contract information (FCI).
implementationGuidance: Implement user account management. Enforce unique user identifiers. Control access to systems through account management and authentication. Maintain list of authorised users.
bestPractices: Implement RBAC for all systems
Review authorised user list quarterly
Automate account deprovisioning upon termination

title: Transaction and Function Control
description: Limit information system access to the types of transactions and functions that authorised users are permitted to execute.
purpose: Restricts what actions authorised users can perform to only those needed for their role.
implementationGuidance: Implement role-based access that restricts users to only the system functions required for their job. Apply least privilege principles. Control execution of privileged functions.
bestPractices: Define roles based on job function
Restrict administrative functions to dedicated admin accounts
Review function authorisations annually

title: Control CUI Flow
description: Control the flow of CUI in accordance with approved authorisations.
purpose: Prevents CUI from flowing to unauthorised systems or individuals.
implementationGuidance: Implement data flow controls that restrict CUI to authorised destinations. Use DLP tools to detect and prevent unauthorised CUI transmission. Define and enforce approved CUI flow paths.
bestPractices: Map all approved CUI flows and block all others
Implement DLP for CUI detection and prevention
Review CUI flow authorisations when systems change

title: Separation of Duties
description: Separate the duties of individuals to reduce the risk of malevolent activity without collusion.
purpose: Prevents any single individual from having sufficient access to commit fraud or error without detection.
implementationGuidance: Identify functions requiring separation of duties. Implement technical and procedural controls to enforce SOD. Assign different individuals to different phases of critical transactions.
bestPractices: Map all SOD requirements before assigning roles
Automate SOD conflict detection
Implement compensating controls where SOD cannot be fully enforced

title: Least Privilege
description: Employ the principle of least privilege, including for specific security functions and privileged accounts.
purpose: Limits the potential damage from mistakes, malicious activity, or system compromise by restricting access to only what is needed.
implementationGuidance: Grant users only the minimum access required to perform their job functions. Implement separate privileged accounts for administrative tasks. Review and reduce excessive privileges regularly.
evidenceExamples: Access review records showing privilege reduction
bestPractices: Use separate accounts for privileged tasks
Remove admin rights from standard user accounts
Review all privileged accounts quarterly

title: Non-Privileged Account Use
description: Use non-privileged accounts or roles when accessing non-security functions.
purpose: Reduces risk from privileged account misuse by requiring non-privileged accounts for routine activities.
implementationGuidance: Require administrators to use non-privileged accounts for everyday tasks and only elevate to privileged accounts when performing administrative functions. Implement just-in-time privileged access.
evidenceExamples: Non-privileged account for daily use evidence
bestPractices: Require all admins to have separate privileged and non-privileged accounts
Implement PAM solution for privileged access management
Log and alert on all privileged account use

title: Privileged Functions
description: Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions and capture the execution of such functions in audit logs.
purpose: Ensures privileged operations are restricted to authorised accounts and fully audited.
implementationGuidance: Configure systems to require privileged credentials for administrative functions. Log all execution of privileged functions. Review privileged function execution logs regularly.
evidenceExamples: System configuration preventing non-privileged users from admin functions
Log review records for privileged functions
bestPractices: Use PAM tools to manage and monitor privileged function execution
Review privileged function logs daily
Alert on unexpected privileged function execution

title: Control Remote Access
description: Monitor and control remote access sessions.
purpose: Ensures remote access to systems containing CUI is controlled and monitored.
implementationGuidance: Implement remote access solutions with strong authentication and encryption. Log all remote access sessions. Monitor for unusual remote access patterns. Terminate inactive remote sessions automatically.
bestPractices: Require MFA for all remote access
Use encrypted VPN for all remote access to CUI systems
Log and review all remote access sessions

title: Remote Access Confidentiality
description: Employ cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions.
purpose: Protects CUI from interception during remote access sessions.
implementationGuidance: Require encrypted remote access sessions using FIPS-validated cryptography. Use VPN with AES-256. Prohibit unencrypted remote connections to CUI systems.
bestPractices: Use FIPS 140-2/3 validated cryptography for remote access
Regularly test remote access encryption
Block all unencrypted remote access protocols (Telnet, FTP)

title: Literacy Training and Awareness
description: Ensure that organisational personnel are aware of the security risks associated with their activities and of the applicable policies, standards, and procedures related to the security of organisational systems.
purpose: Ensures all personnel understand security risks and their obligations.
implementationGuidance: Implement annual security awareness training for all personnel. Cover CUI handling, phishing, social engineering, and security policies. Track completion. Provide role-specific additional training.
bestPractices: Train all personnel annually
Include CUI-specific training for personnel handling FCI/CUI
Use phishing simulations to test awareness

title: Role-Based Training
description: Ensure that personnel are trained to carry out their assigned information security responsibilities.
purpose: Provides role-specific training for personnel with security responsibilities.
implementationGuidance: Identify all personnel with security-specific responsibilities. Provide training appropriate to their roles (e.g., incident responders, system administrators, security personnel). Document and track role-based training completion.
bestPractices: Define security roles and map training requirements to each
Require role-specific training before granting privileged access
Review training content annually for relevance

title: System Auditing
description: Create and retain system audit logs and records to the extent needed to enable the monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorised system activity.
purpose: Creates an audit trail for detecting and investigating security events.
implementationGuidance: Enable audit logging on all systems containing CUI. Log user access, privilege use, authentication events, and system changes. Retain logs for minimum 3 years for government contracts. Protect logs from modification.
evidenceExamples: Audit log configuration for all CUI systems
bestPractices: Centralise logs in SIEM for correlation and analysis
Retain audit logs for contract term plus at least 3 years
Protect logs with immutable storage

title: User Accountability
description: Ensure that the actions of individual users can be uniquely traced to those users so they can be held accountable for their actions.
purpose: Enables individual accountability for actions taken on systems containing CUI.
implementationGuidance: Assign unique user identifiers to all users. Prohibit shared accounts. Configure audit logging to capture user identity with each event. Maintain account-to-individual mapping.
evidenceExamples: Audit logs showing user identity for each action
bestPractices: Prohibit all shared accounts on CUI systems
Map all service accounts to responsible individuals
Test audit trail traceability through periodic reviews

title: System Baselining
description: Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of organisational systems (including hardware, software, firmware, and documentation) throughout the respective system development life cycles.
purpose: Provides a known, secure baseline for all systems to detect deviations.
implementationGuidance: Create and maintain configuration baselines for all CUI systems. Document hardware, software, and firmware inventories. Update baselines when changes occur. Compare current configurations against baseline.
bestPractices: Use automated configuration management tools (Ansible, Chef, Puppet)
Conduct weekly automated configuration scans against baseline
Update baselines only through formal change management

title: Security Configuration Enforcement
description: Establish and enforce security configuration settings for information technology products employed in organisational systems.
purpose: Ensures systems are configured securely to minimise vulnerabilities.
implementationGuidance: Apply security configuration settings (hardening) to all systems. Use CIS Benchmarks or DISA STIGs as configuration standards. Enforce settings through technical controls. Monitor for deviations.
evidenceExamples: STIG or CIS Benchmark compliance scan results
bestPractices: Use DISA STIGs or CIS Benchmarks as hardening standards
Automate configuration compliance checking
Remediate configuration deviations within defined SLAs

title: User Identification
description: Identify information system users, processes acting on behalf of users, and devices.
purpose: Ensures all users, processes, and devices are uniquely identified on CUI systems.
implementationGuidance: Assign unique identifiers to all users, service accounts, and devices. Maintain identity records. Prohibit anonymous access to CUI systems.
evidenceExamples: User account inventory with unique IDs
bestPractices: Use IAM solution for centralised identity management
Maintain a comprehensive device inventory
Review identity records quarterly

title: User Authentication
description: Authenticate (or verify) the identities of those users, processes, or devices, as a prerequisite to allowing access to organisational information systems.
purpose: Ensures only verified identities can access systems containing FCI/CUI.
implementationGuidance: Implement authentication for all users before granting system access. Use strong authentication mechanisms. Implement MFA for privileged access and remote access.
evidenceExamples: Authentication configuration for all CUI systems
MFA implementation evidence for privileged and remote access
bestPractices: Require MFA for all CUI system access
Use phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2) for highest-risk access
Eliminate password-only authentication for CUI systems

title: Multi-Factor Authentication
description: Use multifactor authentication for local and network access to privileged accounts and for network access to non-privileged accounts.
purpose: Provides a stronger second factor of authentication for CUI system access.
implementationGuidance: Implement MFA for all privileged account access (local and network). Implement MFA for all network access to non-privileged accounts. Use authenticators meeting NIST SP 800-63B AAL2 requirements.
evidenceExamples: MFA enforcement for non-privileged network access
bestPractices: Use FIDO2/WebAuthn for phishing-resistant MFA
Apply MFA to all access without exception
Monitor for MFA bypass attempts

title: Incident Handling
description: Establish an operational incident-handling capability for organisational systems that includes preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response activities.
purpose: Ensures CUI-affecting security incidents are handled effectively.
implementationGuidance: Develop and implement an incident response plan. Establish an incident response team. Define phases: preparation, detection/analysis, containment, eradication, recovery, post-incident. Test annually. Include CUI-specific incident scenarios.
bestPractices: Test IR plan at least annually with tabletop exercise
Include CUI-specific incident scenarios in testing
Define reporting obligations to DoD for CUI breaches

title: Incident Reporting
description: Track, document, and report incidents to designated officials and/or authorities both internal and external to the organisation.
purpose: Ensures incidents are documented and reported to appropriate authorities including the DoD.
implementationGuidance: Implement incident tracking and documentation procedures. Define internal escalation procedures. Define external reporting obligations (DoD, DIBNet portal for CUI incidents). Report incidents within required timeframes.
bestPractices: Report CUI-related incidents to DoD within 72 hours
Use DIBNet portal for official incident reporting
Document all incident details for post-incident analysis

title: System Maintenance
description: Perform maintenance on organisational systems.
purpose: Ensures systems are maintained to remain secure and functional.
implementationGuidance: Define and implement a maintenance schedule for all CUI systems. Document all maintenance activities. Ensure maintenance personnel are authorised. Review and approve all maintenance performed.
bestPractices: Schedule preventive maintenance on all CUI systems
Log all maintenance activities with timestamps
Review maintenance logs monthly

title: System Maintenance Controls
description: Provide controls on the tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel that conduct maintenance on organisational systems.
purpose: Ensures maintenance activities do not introduce security risks to CUI systems.
implementationGuidance: Approve and control all maintenance tools. Inspect media used for maintenance. Ensure remote maintenance is encrypted and logged. Remove maintenance accounts when no longer needed.
bestPractices: Maintain a list of approved maintenance tools
Encrypt all remote maintenance sessions
Create and remove temporary maintenance accounts for each maintenance event

title: Media Sanitisation
description: Sanitise or destroy information system media before disposal or reuse.
purpose: Prevents recovery of FCI/CUI from disposed or reused media.
implementationGuidance: Sanitise all media containing FCI/CUI before disposal or reuse using NIST SP 800-88 methods. Track all media through its lifecycle. Obtain certificates of destruction for destroyed media.
bestPractices: Apply NIST SP 800-88 Clear, Purge, or Destroy methods based on data sensitivity
Maintain a media disposal log with method and date
Use certified destruction services for physical media

title: Physical Facility Access
description: Limit physical access to organisational information systems to authorised individuals.
purpose: Prevents unauthorised physical access to systems containing FCI.
implementationGuidance: Implement physical access controls for all areas containing FCI systems. Use electronic badge access, keypads, or equivalent. Manage visitor access. Review access rights periodically.
bestPractices: Implement electronic badge access with audit logging
Review physical access rights quarterly
Escort all visitors in areas with FCI systems

title: Personnel Screening
description: Screen individuals prior to authorising access to organisational systems containing CUI.
purpose: Reduces the risk of insider threats by screening personnel before granting CUI access.
implementationGuidance: Conduct background investigations on all personnel before granting access to CUI. Screen to the level commensurate with the sensitivity of the information. Document screening results.
bestPractices: Screen all personnel before granting any CUI access
Consider security clearance requirements for highest sensitivity CUI
Re-screen personnel when moving to higher-risk positions

title: Personnel Termination
description: Ensure that CUI and organisational systems containing CUI are protected during and after personnel actions such as terminations.
purpose: Prevents former employees from retaining access to CUI after termination.
implementationGuidance: Define procedures for immediate access revocation upon termination. Retrieve all CUI assets and access credentials. Conduct exit procedures covering CUI obligations. Brief separating personnel on continuing CUI protection obligations.
bestPractices: Revoke all access on the day of termination
Recover all CUI assets before final departure
Conduct exit interview covering CUI protection obligations

title: Risk Assessments
description: Periodically assess the risk to organisational operations (including mission, functions, image, or reputation), organisational assets, and individuals, resulting from the operation of organisational systems and the associated processing, storage, or transmission of CUI.
purpose: Identifies and prioritises risks to CUI to guide security control investment.
implementationGuidance: Conduct formal risk assessments at least annually. Identify threats to CUI systems, assess likelihood and impact, and determine risk levels. Use results to prioritise security investments. Update when significant changes occur.
evidenceExamples: Risk register with likelihood and impact ratings
bestPractices: Conduct risk assessments using NIST SP 800-30 methodology
Involve CUI system owners in the risk assessment process
Track risk treatment to closure

title: Vulnerability Scanning
description: Scan for vulnerabilities in organisational systems and applications periodically and when new vulnerabilities affecting those systems are identified.
purpose: Identifies technical vulnerabilities in CUI systems for remediation.
implementationGuidance: Conduct automated vulnerability scans on all CUI systems. Scan internet-facing systems at least weekly, internal systems at least monthly. Remediate identified vulnerabilities based on risk. Conduct scans after significant changes.
bestPractices: Scan all CUI systems at minimum monthly
Prioritise vulnerabilities using CVSS and exploitability
Remediate critical vulnerabilities within 24–72 hours

title: Security Assessment
description: Periodically assess the security controls in organisational systems to determine if the controls are effective in their application.
purpose: Validates that implemented security controls are effective and appropriately protecting CUI.
implementationGuidance: Conduct periodic security assessments (at least annually) of all CUI system security controls. Use a consistent assessment methodology. Document findings and implement remediation. Engage a third-party assessor for CMMC L3 assessments.
bestPractices: Conduct annual security assessments
Use an independent assessor for objective results
Track all assessment findings to closure

title: Plan of Action and Milestones (POAM)
description: Develop and implement plans of action designed to correct deficiencies and reduce or eliminate vulnerabilities in organisational systems.
purpose: Provides a structured approach to remediating identified security deficiencies.
implementationGuidance: Document all identified security deficiencies in a Plan of Action and Milestones (POAM). Include planned remediation actions, resources, and milestones. Track POAM items to closure. Report on POAM status regularly.
bestPractices: Maintain a current POAM at all times
Set realistic but aggressive remediation milestones
Review and update POAM at least monthly

title: Boundary Protection
description: Monitor, control, and protect organisational communications at the external boundaries and key internal boundaries of information systems.
purpose: Protects CUI systems at network boundaries using security controls.
implementationGuidance: Deploy firewalls and intrusion detection systems at network boundaries. Deny traffic by default. Monitor all boundary traffic. Segregate CUI systems from general networks.
evidenceExamples: Network segmentation diagram with CUI segment
bestPractices: Implement next-generation firewalls at CDE boundaries
Segment CUI systems into dedicated network zone
Monitor boundary traffic 24/7 with SIEM alerting

title: Data in Transit Protection
description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorised disclosure of CUI during transmission unless otherwise protected by alternative physical safeguards.
purpose: Prevents interception of CUI during network transmission.
implementationGuidance: Encrypt all CUI transmitted over networks using FIPS-validated cryptography. Use TLS 1.2 or higher. Prohibit transmission of unencrypted CUI. Implement encryption for all email containing CUI.
evidenceExamples: TLS configuration for CUI transmission paths
bestPractices: Use FIPS 140-2/3 validated cryptography for all CUI in transit
Implement TLS 1.2+ for all web and API communications
Encrypt email containing CUI using S/MIME or equivalent

title: Flaw Remediation
description: Identify, report, and correct information and information system flaws in a timely manner.
purpose: Ensures vulnerabilities and bugs are identified and fixed to protect CUI systems.
implementationGuidance: Implement vulnerability management covering flaw identification, reporting, and remediation. Define SLAs for remediation by severity. Apply patches within SLAs. Test patches before applying to production.
bestPractices: Patch critical vulnerabilities within 72 hours
Automate patch deployment for standard patches
Test patches in non-production before production deployment

title: Malicious Code Protection
description: Provide protection from malicious code at appropriate locations within organisational information systems.
purpose: Detects and prevents malware from compromising CUI systems.
implementationGuidance: Deploy anti-malware on all CUI systems. Keep signatures updated. Enable real-time protection. Perform regular scans. Configure to alert on detection and take immediate action. Deploy at boundary and host levels.
bestPractices: Achieve 100% anti-malware coverage on CUI systems
Use next-gen EDR with behavioural detection
Monitor anti-malware health centrally with immediate alerts on failures

title: Security Alert Monitoring
description: Monitor organisational systems including inbound and outbound communications traffic to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.
purpose: Enables real-time detection of attacks against CUI systems.
implementationGuidance: Deploy intrusion detection systems and SIEM. Monitor all inbound and outbound network traffic. Define detection rules for known attack patterns and indicators of compromise. Alert and respond to detections promptly.
bestPractices: Implement 24/7 security monitoring for CUI systems
Tune SIEM rules to minimise false positives
Conduct threat hunting to find threats not caught by automated tools
